text.title: Latency vs Quality Decision Grid Example
text.summary: One full grid for a chat-search-summarise app showing per-site routing decisions.

example.code:
# Decision grid — support-app (2026-05-22)
| site | p50 ms | p95 ms | qual floor | model | tactic | $/call | owner | |------------------|--------|--------|------------|--------|-------------------------------|---------|----------| | autocomplete | 200 | 400 | 0.75 | haiku | prompt-cache + tight max_tok | 0.0005 | fe-team | | chat | 1500 | 3000 | 0.88 | sonnet | stream + tools | 0.012 | chat-team| | audit-summary | 12000 | 30000 | 0.95 | opus | structured-output + 2-pass | 0.18 | ops-team |
## Rationale per site
- autocomplete: user-typed flow, must feel instant; quality floor is "completion not embarrassing"; haiku + prompt-cache hits both axes. - chat: streaming hides latency, quality matters more than absolute speed; sonnet sweet-spot. - audit-summary: offline-feel, user expects a wait; opus + structured-output justifies cost via downstream legal use.
## Rollback triggers (auto-revert via flag) - autocomplete: p95 > 600 ms for 15 min OR quality drop > 0.05 - chat: p95 > 4000 ms for 15 min OR quality drop > 0.03 - audit-summary: cost per call > $0.30 OR quality drop > 0.02
example.note: Three distinct sites, three different models, three different rollback triggers — that is the whole point of the grid.
